mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 equities and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, consumer spending power contracts sharply, suppressing corporate revenue growth and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of months where UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 3.0 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month through earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns when either series shows month-over-month growth below 0.5 percent, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in the index of industrial production reflects immediate capacity utilization improvements, triggering short-term positive equity momentum over the next week as traders price in near-term output gains.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during supply-constrained periods where input costs rise faster than output, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of acceleration months.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 225.0, rising input costs compress corporate profit margins and increase the probability of restrictive monetary policy, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Corporate margins expand despite CPIAUCSL exceeding 225.0, or forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered months over a rolling 5-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening, which forces upward earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict returns when wage growth remains below 2.0 percent annually, or the correlation between the joint signal and forward returns drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly translates to higher aggregate corporate revenues, establishing a persistent positive trend in S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth fails to translate to earnings growth due to margin compression, resulting in negative forward returns in >50% of expansion quarters over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the industrial production index below 95.0 signals immediate manufacturing contraction and supply chain disruptions, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as risk aversion spikes.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of months where INDPRO falls below 95.0, or the signal is consistently overridden by concurrent monetary easing announcements.
